questions.q: What is the main purpose of the three-tier system of panchayati raj institutions?
questions.answer: To ensure effective and efficient decentralization of power.

questions.q: Who is the head of the Gram Panchayat?
What is the role of the Zila Parishad in the context of panchayati raj institutions?
questions.answer: The Zila Parishad acts as a link between the district administration and the Gram Panchayats. It is responsible for preparing the district plan, allocating funds, and overseeing the implementation of development schemes at the district level.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of panchayati raj institutions?
What is the significance of the Seventy-Third and Seventy-Fourth Amendments to the Constitution?
questions.answer: These amendments provide for the establishment of the three-tier system of panchayats and introduce reservation for women and SC/ST candidates.

questions.q: Which of the following is a function of the Gram Panchayat?
questions.answer: Providing basic amenities such as water, sanitation, and electricity to the rural masses

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'gram swaraj' as envisioned by Mahatma Gandhi.
questions.answer: Gram swaraj refers to the idea of self-rule or self-governance at the village level. Gandhi envisioned a decentralized system of governance where power is held by the local community, and decisions are made through consensus and participation. The goal is to create a self-sufficient and harmonious rural community.

questions.q: Who is responsible for the implementation of the 73rd Amendment to the Constitution?
What is the main objective of the Panchayati Raj system?
questions.answer: To promote participatory democracy and ensure the involvement of the local community in the decision-making process.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of the three-tier system of panchayati raj institutions?
questions.answer: Decentralization of power and increased participation of citizens
